text: on January 19 2010 agents posing as
tourists targeted Hamas Commander Mahmud
al- Mambu two of the agents disguised as
tennis players followed him to his floor
in a five-star hotel in Dubai in what
police say was an attempt to note down
his room number hours later surveillance
footage captured a man and a woman
standing guard in the hallway as the
execution team ambushed Al Mambu in his
room the agents swiftly left Dubai and
evaded capture leading to widespread
allegations that the operation was
orchestrated by Israel and executed by
mad mad was founded in 1949 the year
after deid Boran proclaimed the
establishment of the state of Israel
after the horrors of the Holocaust A UN
resolution in 1947 proposed the
partition of Palestine allocating 56% of
the land to the Jewish state which made
up about 13d of the total population six
Arab states declared war on Israel
vowing to crush the Jew Jewish State the
Israelis won the war in the aftermath
Jordan occupied the West Bank and East
Jerusalem Egypt occupied the Gaza Strip
and Israel ended up with 78% of the land
Prime Minister David Boran understood
that the key to Israel's survival lay in
robust intelligence capabilities Israel
has three intelligence agencies Aman is
responsible for military intelligence
gathering information about enemy armies
and other military threats shinb handles
internal security focusing on preventing
terrorism and Espionage within Israel
mad conducts foreign intelligence
operations specializing in Espionage
covert missions and counterterrorism
outside Israel recruiters look for
candidates with specific qualities
reliability exceptional intelligence and
the ability to become somebody else
entirely mad reportedly employs about
7,000 people globally making it one of
the world's largest ESP
agencies David Boran never uttered the
names of Israel's intelligence agencies
never acknowledged that they existed
they were shrouded in secrecy that is
until the case of Adolf ikman one of the
architects of the Holocaust ikan was
captured by mad agents in buono Aries in
1961 where he had been living under the
Alias Ricardo CL mad captured him and
brought him to Israel to stand trial
where he was sentenced to death the
operation put mad on the map and made
headlines around the world sending a
clear message that Israeli agents could
strike anywhere but targeting Nazi
officials was not mad's main mission its
main focus was on safeguarding the state
of Israel against its enemies in the
summer of 1962 Egypt's President Gamal
abdal Naser proudly declared that his
Rockets were capable of reaching Israel
the agent mad planted in Cairo wolf King
Lots who posed as a German horse breeder
had failed to report the major progress
in Egypt's missile program nasser's
missile program relied heavily on
expertise from former Nazi scientists
like Hines Krug who managed Logistics in
an effort to derail the program mad
targeted key figures with parcel bombs
that killed several Egyptian workers but
none of the major players when Krug
returned to Munich mad agents kidnapped
him and took him to Israel to be
interrogated he was never seen alive
again but Egypt's missile program was
still very much alive so in May 19 64
mad took an unconventional approach to
sabotage the program by making a deal
with former SS officer AOS sceni he was
a prominent Nazi who had Direct access
to Leading figures in Egypt's rocket
program mad promised protection in
exchange for his cooperation by
leveraging his contacts scorten
persuaded one German scientist after
another to leave Egypt an Egypt's
missile program came to a halt however
Egypt soon found a new source of support
as Soviet weapons began flowing into
neighboring Arab countries during the
Cold War to counter Western influence in
the region the delivery of the modern
Soviet combat jet the mig21 to Arab
states alarmed Israel mad devised a plan
to find an Arab pilot willing to defect
and fly one of the Jets to them they
persuaded munir Reda an Assyrian
Christian in the Iraqi Air Force who
faced discrimination to defect by
offering him money and protection Reda
agreed on one condition he wanted his
entire extended family all 21 people to
be smuggled out of Iraq Israel agreed in
Operation Diamond successfully brought a
mig21 into Western hands leveraging its
Superior intelligence Israel launched a
preemptive strike against Egypt Jordan
and Syria on June 5 1967 in response to
mobilization by its enemies Israeli
forces defeated the combined Arab armies
in six days Israel took control of the
West Bank from Jordan the Gaza Strip in
Sinai Peninsula from Egypt and the golen
Heights from Syria humiliating its Arab
neighbors by the end of the Six Day War
Israel controlled four times as much
land as it did in
1948 thousands of refugees left their
homes in the wake of the six- day war to
seek shelter in the Arab held lands east
of the river for many of them Jordan had
nothing to offer but the squalor and the
misery of the refugee camps young
Palestinians growing up in refugee camps
became increasingly radicalized a
development mad failed to anticipate the
anger led to the rise of militant groups
such as fah which eventually became the
dominant faction within the Palestine
Liberation Organization yaser Arafat who
founded fah vowed to wipe Israel off the
map mad tried time and time again to
assassinate Arafat but he managed to
evade capture by hiding in neighboring
Arab countries Arafat became the
chairman of the PLO in 1969 and focused
on radicalizing Palestinians in Exile in
Europe establishing networks in
universities and workingclass
neighborhoods in the 60s and 70s the PLO
carried out numerous terrorist attacks
in Europe and Israel to draw attention
to the Palestinian cause including the
shocking attack on Israeli athletes at
the 1972 Olympic Games in Munich on
September 5 1972 eight men with a Black
September organization in offshoot of
fah infiltrated the Olympic Village and
took 11 Israeli athletes hostage the
terrorists demanded the release of over
200 prisoners held in Israeli jails
Israeli Prime Minister Golda mayir
rejected the demands and instead offered
to help Germany rescue the hostages
however Germany declined insisting on
handling the situation themselves mad's
hands were often tied as European
intelligence Services prefer to remain
neutral in the Israeli Palestinian
conflict the German authorities
pretended to agree to the terrorist
demands to be flown to an Arab friendly
country the plan was to Stage a rescue
operation at the first and feldberg
Airbase however it went horribly wrong
as German police were not trained
snipers and were using outdated
equipment old rifles without Scopes all
11 Israeli athletes were killed in
response mad vowed to track down and
eliminate those
responsible within a year mad operatives
tracked down and killed Palestinians in
Rome Paris Beirut Athens in Cyprus but
it also made mistakes like in Norway
when mad agents mistakenly killed a
Moroccan waiter whom they misidentified
as Ali Hassan salame the alleged
Mastermind of the Munich attack mad
ended up taking out salame in a car bomb
years later mad strategy extended Beyond
retaliation and included fostering
Regional alliances exemplified by the
adage my enemy's enemy is my friend
these connections proved invaluable when
Israelis were in danger far from home on
June 27
1976 a splinter group of the PLO
hijacked an Air France jet with 260
people on board and rerouted it to
Uganda the hijackers released passengers
who didn't appear to be Jewish but
continued to hold 106 others including
crew members demanding the release of
Palestinian prisoners although uganda's
dictator Ed Amin was no longer Israel's
Ally Uganda had in the past provided
Israel with critical Intelligence on the
airport's facilities an Israeli company
had built the airport giving Israel
access to detailed Blueprints and a
Massad agent posing as an amateur pilot
had recently taken aerial photographs of
the airport operation in TBE led by
Jonathan Netanyahu the brother of future
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin
Netanyahu was an outrageously bold
operation the rescue team flew in a
plane camouflage to look like a
commercial aircraft to avoid protection
the aircraft refueled in Kenya another
crucial Ally whose support was vital for
the mission's success during the raid
Jonathan n yahu was killed along with 45
Ugandan soldiers and three hostages who
died in the crossfire one rescued
hostage Dora Blau who was later
hospitalized after choking on a chicken
bone was shot dead on Ed Amin's orders
despite the losses the operation was a
success underscoring mad's ability to
leverage its intelligence networks in
alliances to execute daring
operations but mad's reputation would
soon take a hit in 1970 Egypt saw a new
president come into power anoir saddat
saddad publicly threatened to reclaim
the Sinai Peninsula but Israel didn't
take him seriously mad's informant
Ashraf Marwan who was married to the
daughter of Egypt's former president
Naser had not alerted them to the threat
some believe Marwan became an informant
because of the disrespect he felt from
ner who allegedly disapproved of the
marriage in the fall of 1973 Israel's
Military Intelligence observed Egyptian
in Syrian troop movements along the
borders but dismissed them as routine
believing Marwan would have warned them
if an attack were imminent when Marwan
did send an Urgent Message warning that
Egypt planned to attack within hours on
yum kapor the holiest day in the Jewish
calendar mad was skeptical Israel's
hesitation proved costly although Israel
W the Young Kapur War it suffered
massive losses over 2,600 Israeli
soldiers were killed marwan's identity
as a mad informant was exposed 30 years
later in 2007 he mysteriously fell to
his death from his balcony in London in
the Years following the war Egypt and
Israel began to mend their relationship
by negotiating peace with Israel Egypt
aimed to improve its relations with the
US a key Ally of Israel and break away
from Soviet influence during secret
negotiations Israel's foreign minister
OSHA Dian made a covert trip to Morocco
where he was seen wearing dark
sunglasses instead of his trademark
black eye patch later inir Sadat became
the first Arab head of state to visit
Israel and speak before the nesset
Israel's House of Representatives US
President Jimmy Carter hosted the tough
talks between Israeli Prime Minister
mahim bean and Egyptian president Al
sadad resulting in the Camp David
Accords in the signing of a historic
peace treat on March 26th
1979 Israel agreed to withdraw from the
Sinai Peninsula Egypt officially
recognize the state of Israel becoming
the first Arab country to do so the
landmark agreement shocked the Arab
world Sadat's bold move led to Egypt's
expulsion from the Arab League and
tragically his assassination 3 years
later the treaty marked a pivotal moment
in Middle Eastern history showcasing
mad's ability to not only wage war but
broke her peace however the regional
landscape shifted dramatically with the
Advent of the Iranian Revolution Iran
had been one of Israel's secret allies
since the 1950s under the rule of sha
Muhammed Rea Pahlavi Iran sold oil to
Israel and in return Massad provided
internal security support and Military
Intelligence training then in 1978 huge
protests erupted led by Islamic cleric
Ayatollah Ki who was in Exile first in
Iraq and then in Paris the Sha facing
mounting pressure appealed to Mad to
assassinate Ki but Israel refused out of
concern for regional stability
eventually the revolution broke out and
Iran's secret police lost control the
Sha fled the country and Kini returned
to establish a theocracy executing
military generals and solidifying his
rule Iran once Israel's critical partner
in the region now became its greatest
adversary Iran openly declared nited
goal of annihilating Israel which
delighted the PLO under yaser Arafat who
found common cause with the new Iranian
regime in their opposition to both the
US and
Israel mad aimed to dismantle the PLO
which had established a base in Lebanon
after being expelled from Jordan
Palestinian refugee camps in southern
Lebanon were increasingly infiltrating
Israeli territory Israeli Prime Minister
manaheim bean and defense minister Ariel
Chiron aimed to purge the the PLO from
Lebanon with a military strike however
they needed to be able to justify
invading a foreign country the attempted
assassination of Israel's ambassador to
the UK schlomo argov by a radical
Palestinian faction provided them with
an excuse despite the PLO not being
involved mad allied with lebanon's
Christian militias which saw the PLO as
a mutual enemy though mad had doubts
about their reliability on June 6th 1982
Israeli soldiers invaded Lebanon on in
hopes of destroying the PLO and
installing a pro-israeli regime 2 months
after the invasion the PLO retreated and
Basher jayel leader of the Christian
militias became the new president
however his assassination 3 months later
plunged Lebanon back into Civil War
which confirmed mad's fears about the
Christian militia's reliability the
catastrophe in Lebanon forced prime
minister bean and defense minister
Chiron to resign with mad also facing
criticism from misjudging the
situation mad's mission extended Beyond
Warfare it was committed to protecting
Jewish lives wherever they were in Peril
in the early 1980s tens of thousands of
Ethiopian Jews were at risk of violence
and hunger thousands had fled to refugee
camps in neighboring Sudan thead was
tasked with bringing them to Israel but
the operation had to be carried out in
secret as Sudan's dictator Jafar animi
needed to maintain a public stance of
opposition to Israel to avoid political
backlash mad operatives disguised as
European and American Staff set up a
diving Resort on the Sudanese Coast
where they managed it by day and
smuggled Jews out by Night transporting
them by boat across the Red Sea to
Israel when smuggling by boat became too
risky the refugees were secretly
airlifted out of the country new
challenges soon emerged for Israel in
the late 1980s anger in Gaza which
Israel had occupied for over 20 years
erupted into violence this led to a mass
Uprising called the inata which caught
mad offg guard as the violence escalated
and Israel intensified its efforts to
suppress the Palestinian Uprising
demands for a diplomatic solution grew
louder in Israel in 1993 Israelis and
Palestinians met on neutral ground in
Oslo Norway to discuss a possible
compromise Israel agre withdraw its
troops from Gaza in parts of the West
Bank transferring these territories to
Palestinian self-government remarkably
Yasir Arafat the head of the PLO who had
earlier vowed to wipe Israel off the map
now recognized Israel's right to exist
the peace treaty was signed in
Washington and both Israeli Prime
Minister yitzak Rabin and Arafat
received the Nobel Peace Prize the
following year however the peace process
faced significant challenges Arafat no
longer spoke for all Palestinians and
the PLO struggled to maintain peace as a
new r radical Force emerge founded by
shik Ahmed Yasin during the intifada
Hamas remains steadfast in its goal of
destroying Israel Israel had expected
the PLO to reign in Hamas but the PLO
was slow to act allowing Hamas to grow
stronger and launch a devastating wave
of suicide bombings against Israeli
cities in 1996 Israel's new prime
minister Benjamin n yahu came to power
with a mandate to pursue a policy of
strength then yahu instructed mad to
Target key Hamas leaders in order to
stop the attacks in the summer of 1997
mad's first Target was khed michall the
then chief of hamas's poit Bureau in
Aman Jordan the plan was to poison him
in broad daylight to make it appear as
though he died of natural causes
avoiding disrupting the good
relationship between Israel and Jordan
as michall walked with his children in
bodyguard two mad agents posing as
Canadian tourists sprayed fentanyl in
his ears
however Mall's bodyguard intervened and
the Mad agents were exposed and captured
King Hussein o Jordan was appalled by
the incident and warned that if michall
died he would face a Palestinian
Uprising and have no choice but to
execute the Israeli operatives Israel
agreed to provide the antidote to save
Mall's life khed Mall survived and
became hamas's new leader suicide
attacks in Israel persisted culminating
in the second inata which saw a
significant rise in violence as Israel
faced ongoing attacks a new and Powerful
adversary emerged Iran was frustrated by
its exclusion from Middle East peace
negotiations it increased its Aid to
Hamas in Palestinian Islamic Jihad and
established a network among Israel's
enemies in 2002 Meer Dean the new head
of mad identified Iran as Israel's main
adversary he reformed the agency with a
more aggressive approach under de
leadership mad focused on dismantling
anti-israeli Terror networks and
countering Iran's nuclear program Iran
maintained its nuclear program was
strictly for civilian purposes but
Israel perceived a significant threat
mad analysts believed that Iran could
develop nuclear weapons within a few
years Israel wasn't going to let that
happen it was the only country in the
Middle East with nuclear weapons and
wanted to keep it that way but mad ruled
out an air strike on Iran's nuclear
facilities as it could have drastic
repercussions instead it tried to
Iran's nuclear program with a
computer worm the covert operation
believed to involve the CIA and mad
spread the stuck net worm to computers
at Iran's Nan's nuclear facility stuck
net targeted the centrifuges that
produced enriched uranium causing them
to burn out I did a video on stuck net
which I've Linked In My description the
operation disrupted Iran's nuclear
program but concerns remained leading to
this famous 2012 UN speech by Netanyahu
where should a red line be
drawn a red line should be drawn right
here in 2015 Iran signed a long-term
agreement with the US under the Obama
Administration to curb its nuclear
activity in exchange for sanctions
relief however in 2018 mad obtained
documents that allegedly showed Iran was
still pursuing its nuclear program
leading president Trump to cancel the
agreement
since World War II Israel has reportedly
been responsible for more assassinations
than any other Western Country with
estimates suggesting it has carried out
at least
assassinations yet there are limits to
Israel's intelligence gathering on
October 7 2023 during a music festival
in southern Israel Hamas launched a
surprise attack from the Gaza Strip
killing 364 People Israel and Hamas
engaged in intense fighting resulting in
thousands of casualties the surprise
attack represented a stunning failure of
Israeli intelligence which has been
crucial to Israel's strength in survival
until now the reasons behind the lapse
will be the subject of intense scrutiny
and Analysis by Israel and its allies
for years to
come thanks for watching for news think
I'm Cindy Palm
[Music]
